meta.title: Animalism
meta.description:
Join the Nobodies Collective to support this project as a producer (your name in the film’s credits!) and gain access to tons of exclusive resources: videos, documents, tutorials, and much more.
👉 Be part of it here: 
https://www.patreon.com/c/NobodyandTheComputer

🐾 Discover How I Made This AI-Generated Film! 🎬
In this book, I share the full creative process, starting from the original idea, concept development, and screenplay to the AI tools used for generating images, videos, sound, and music. Plus, you'll get access to the prompts I used for Midjourney and Kling.ai.
📖 Get it here: 
https://www.patreon.com/posts/animalism-ai-122718150

00:00 Chimpanzees go wild
02:15 Koalas call the cops
03:20 Police dogs
05:06 Poco Loco Pizzeria
05:51 Yogui the cat
06:11 Dogs and pigs
07:49 Koalas, dogs, and piglets
08:22 Dogs watching pink elephants
09:01 The End






Philosophically, animalism is the idea that humans are fundamentally animals, a theory in personal identity that contrasts with mind-body dualism and John Locke’s psychological criterion. Thinkers like Eric T. Olson, Peter van Inwagen, Paul Snowdon, and David Hershenov have explored this perspective.
On a social level, animalism also refers to a movement that considers non-human animals to be morally and ethically significant, challenging the way we view our relationship with nature.
